title: Dead Code Elimination
concept: Prompting coding agents to identify and prune unused imports, obsolete helper functions, and dead logic blocks.

exercises.prompt: What does 'dead code' refer to in programming?

options.text: Code that is helpful and necessary.
Code that is never run or used.
Code that has a lot of comments.

exercises.explain: Dead code refers to parts of the code that are never executed, which can slow down programs and make them messy.

cards.title: Understanding Dead Code
cards.body: Dead code is like having extra branches on a tree that don't grow any leaves. They take up space but don't contribute anything useful.

cards.title: What is Dead Code?
cards.body: Dead code includes unused imports, outdated helper functions, and logic that will never run. It can make the code harder to read.

cards.title: The Untended Garden
cards.body: Think of a garden with many dead plants. Just like it's difficult to see the healthy ones, dead code clutters programming, making it hard to manage.

cards.title: Example of Dead Code
cards.body: If you have a function that checks for errors, but you never call this function in your program, that’s dead code.

cards.title: Myth: All Code is Good Code
cards.body: Some believe that all written code is always good. However, dead code can actually confuse programmers and slow down performance.

cards.title: Identifying Dead Code
cards.body: See how this code looks with and without dead code?

left.text: Function checkData() is declared, but never called.

right.text: Function checkData() is removed and the code is cleaner.

exercises.prompt: What is the first step in eliminating dead code?

options.text: Add more code to the project.
Identify code that is not used.
Run all the code to check for errors.

exercises.explain: Identifying unused or useless code is the first crucial step in clean code practices.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Dead code can sometimes be helpful in programming.
exercises.explain: Dead code does not help and should be removed to improve code quality.

exercises.prompt: Pruning dead code helps make your code more ______.
exercises.sentence: Pruning dead code helps make your code more readable.
exercises.explain: Making code readable means it's easier for others to understand and work with.

exercises.prompt: Which option describes a task for coding agents during dead code elimination?

options.text: Write new features for the application.
Identify and remove code that is never executed.
Create new functions to replace old ones.

exercises.explain: The main goal during this process is to identify and remove code that will never run, making the program cleaner.

exercises.prompt: What is the most important benefit of removing dead code from a program?

options.text: It makes the program run faster.
It adds more features to the program.
It reduces the number of comments needed.

exercises.explain: Removing dead code helps make the program more efficient and easier to understand, enabling it to run faster and preventing any unnecessary clutter.
